title: Java Strings
content:
### Strings in Java

Strings are sequences of characters used to represent text.

---

### Creating Strings

```java
String str1 = "Hello";              // String literal
String str2 = new String("Hello");  // Using new keyword
```

---

### String Methods

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `length()` | Returns string length |
| `charAt()` | Character at index |
| `substring()` | Extract substring |
| `indexOf()` | Find first occurrence |
| `toUpperCase()` | Convert to uppercase |
| `toLowerCase()` | Convert to lowercase |
| `replace()` | Replace characters |
| `split()` | Split into array |
| `equals()` | Compare strings |
| `trim()` | Remove whitespace |

---
### String Concatenation

```java
String first = "John";
String last = "Doe";
String full = first + " " + last;     // Using +
String full2 = first.concat(" ").concat(last);  // Using concat()
```

---

### String Comparison

| Method | Behavior |
|--------|----------|
| `equals()` | Compares content (case-sensitive) |
| `equalsIgnoreCase()` | Compares content (case-insensitive) |
| `compareTo()` | Lexicographic comparison |

> **Key Point:** Strings are immutable in Java. Any operation that modifies a string creates a new object. Use `StringBuilder` or `StringBuffer` for mutable strings.
